mnemonics: Standard Error is the Standard Deviation of the Sampling Distribution (SESD)
keyPoints: Standard error measures the variability of a sample statistic (like the mean) from sample to sample.
It is calculated by dividing the population standard deviation (σ) by the square root of the sample size (n): SE = σ/√n.
A smaller standard error indicates that the sample statistic is a more precise estimate of the population parameter.
commonMistakes: Confusing standard error (SE) with standard deviation (SD); SD describes variability in the data, while SE describes the precision of an estimate.
Forgetting to use the sample standard deviation (s) as an estimate for σ when the population parameter is unknown: SE ≈ s/√n.
